headline: Uber Robotaxi Partnerships Grow As Valuation Lags Analyst Expectations
summary: Uber Technologies (NYSE:UBER) has started live commercial robotaxi rides with Motional in Las Vegas, matching riders with autonomous vehicles through the standard Uber app. The company has expanded its autonomous vehicle partnerships, adding Zoox in the US and launching a pilot robotaxi program with Wayve and Nissan in Tokyo. These deployments are unfolding alongside new regulatory and legal developments, including a US class action lawsuit related to time-based pricing and user-facing...

These deployments are unfolding alongside new regulatory and legal developments, including a US class action lawsuit related to time-based pricing and user-facing...

headline: Why The Uber Technologies (UBER) Story Is Evolving With AV Partnerships And Trimmed Valuations
summary: Uber Technologies has seen its fair value estimate nudged from US$104.47 to US$103.81 per share, a subtle reset that keeps the focus on how analysts are fine tuning their models rather than rewriting the story. Recent commentary links these changes to a mix of enthusiasm around autonomous partnerships, AI driven efficiencies, and capital returns, set against more cautious views on valuation and sector wide risks. Read on to see what is driving these moves and how you can keep track of the...

headline: Will Multi‑State eVTOL Pilots and FAA Test Milestones Change Joby Aviation's (JOBY) Narrative?
summary: Earlier this month, Joby Aviation announced it had completed piloted electric air taxi demonstration flights across the San Francisco Bay Area and begun flight testing its first FAA-conforming aircraft, while also being chosen as a partner in the White House-backed eVTOL Integration Pilot Program to start early operations across 10 U.S. states. These developments highlight how Joby is pairing regulatory progress with real-world urban flight demonstrations and an integrated ecosystem with...

headline: Travis Kalanick sees benefits of being in stealth mode for 8 years. ‘You build a culture of people that want to build and do not need to be famous’
summary: Uber cofounder Travis Kalanick unveiled a robotics company for the food, mining and transport industries.

content.title: Travis Kalanick sees benefits of being in stealth mode for 8 years. ‘You build a culture of people that want to build and do not need to be famous’
